title: Uniface Messages 2000-2999
text: Uniface Messages 2000-2999 Refer to the Uniface message number, message text, and an explanation of the message. The following table refers to messages with numbering between 2000 - 2999 . Search: Column Message number Message text Explanation Filter results... 40 rows found Message number Message text Explanation Filter 40 rows found Filter 40 rows found Filter 40 rows found Message number Message text Explanation 2002 I/O error detected. This error can occur under the following conditions: Uniface received an I/O error message from the DBMS. Uniface generates the return code -2 in $status . Check the message frame for the DBMS error message. The ProcScript in the leaveModifiedKey trigger returns a negative value to $status . This value is always set to -2. For example, an occurrence of the many entity is removed after retrieve/o is issued and before retrieve/e is issued. 2003 Cannot open file. Uniface received an error message from the DBMS during an open operation. Uniface generates the return code -4 in $status . Check the message frame for a DBMS error. This message is also generated when you try to print to a file that does not exist or is in a protected directory. 2004 No modifications allowed on occurrences of entity %%$entname. You tried to modify an occurrence in an entity that does not allow updates. Uniface generates the return code -5 in $status . This message is displayed when the entity is set to "No Updates" in the component or the model. To allow updates, change the setting using the the Define Component Entity Properties form or the Define Entity form, then recompile the component. 2006 Illegal duplicate key. An attempt was made to store a duplicate key value in an index field that is defined as unique. Uniface generates the return code -7 in $status . If you require duplicate key values, change the application model and DBMS definitions. Otherwise, store unique key values only. 2008 Occurrence cannot be modified due to fetch error. This is a locking problem. Try again later. 2009 Occurrence locked. The occurrence you tried to lock is already locked by another user. Uniface generates the return code -11 in $status . 2012 Occurrence mismatch When Uniface tried to lock the occurrence, a difference was detected between the occurrence in the component and the occurrence in the database. Another user may have modified the occurrence, or perhaps the structural I/O definitions do not exactly match the database. Uniface generates the return code -10 in $status . To help identify the component and entity where the error occurred, a call stack is generated. For example: Explain this code 10/03/2011 10:18:22] [ADMSSV - DELE: 1] 2012 - occurrence mismatch. Call stack: _delete, DELE:1, [delete ] Comp: ADMSSV/_I00000485 OPER, SAVE:1, [store/e ENT_NAME] Comp: ADMSSV/_I00000485 OPER, LOGOFF:32, [call SAVE("STATE")] Comp: ADMSSV/_I00000485 OPER, SWITCH:43, [activate/stateless "ADMSSV"."LOGOFF"( P1 , P2 , P3 , P4 ) ] Comp: ADMSSV OPER, WRDEXEC:214, [activate "%%INSTANCE"."%%OPERATION"( ) ] Comp: USYSHTTP/_I00000482 2012 - occurrence mismatch in component [ADMSSV], entity [STATE], see message frame for more information. Copy When you receive the return code -10 in $status , retrieve again or reload in the lock trigger. The new occurrence overwrites the old occurrence. 2013 Occurrence does not exist. After your last retrieve, the occurrence you tried to modify was deleted from the database. Uniface generates the return code -10 in $status . ^RETRIEVE again, or specify reload in the lock trigger when you receive the return code -10 in $status . 2017 Error opening file %%$sub for writing. The file named could not be opened for writing. Check the file protection settings. 2018 Error writing to file %%$sub%%%. Uniface detected an error while writing to a file. Check that the file system or disk is not full. 2019 Error opening file %%$sub for reading. This file could not be opened for reading. Check the file protection settings. 2020 Error reading from file %%$sub%%%. Uniface detected an error while reading from a file. This can be due to a system I/O error. 2101 Requested function not supported by DBMS. You tried to use a function not supported by the DBMS that manages the entity. 2200 Key not found; specify existing key. This is a default message used in the Leave Modified Key trigger. It appears when retrieve/o returns -2 in $status , and indicates that an occurrence with the current primary key values was not found. Enter a valid primary key. 2201 Key not found; new record assumed. This is a default message used in the Leave Modified Key trigger. It appears when retrieve/o returns 1 in $status , and indicates that an occurrence with the current primary key values was not found. You are editing an entity that contains a foreign key in which the writeUp trigger contains code. A new occurrence is inserted when you ^STORE. 2202 More hits available. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns -15 in $status , and indicates that several occurrences with the current primary key values were found. Enter a valid primary key that matches one database occurrence. 2203 Occurrence unremoved. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns 3 in $status , and indicates that an occurrence with the current primary key values was found among the occurrences marked for deletion. The occurrence is unremoved, and the cursor is positioned on that occurrence. Continue editing. 2204 Key found in form; occurrence made current. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns 4 in $status , and indicates that an occurrence with the current primary key values was found in the form. The cursor is scrolled forward or backward to that occurrence. Continue editing. 2205 Key is ambiguous. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns -14 in $status , and indicates that several occurrences with the current primary key values were found in the form. Enter a valid primary key value. 2206 Key not complete. You tried to store an entity with a foreign key, but the compound key for that entity is only partially filled in. Specify the missing key fields. 2300 Argument %%$sub%%% is not a file or directory name. The argument must be a file name or directory name. 2301 Existence check failed for %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) The specified file or directory does not exist. 2302 Cannot create directory %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) ldircreate could not create the specified directory. 2303 Cannot delete directory %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) ldirdelete could not delete the specified directory. 2204 Key found in form; occurrence made current. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns 4 in $status , and indicates that an occurrence with the current primary key values was found in the form. The cursor is scrolled forward or backward to that occurrence. Continue editing. 2205 Key is ambiguous. This is one of the default messages used in the leaveModifiedKey trigger. It is displayed when retrieve/o returns -14 in $status , and indicates that several occurrences with the current primary key values were found in the form. Enter a valid primary key value. 2206 Key not complete. You tried to store an entity with a foreign key, but the compound key for that entity is only partially filled in. Specify the missing key fields. 2300 Argument %%$sub%%% is not a file or directory name. The argument must be a file name or directory name. 2301 Existence check failed for %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) The specified file or directory does not exist. 2302 Cannot create directory %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) ldircreate could not create the specified directory. 2303 Cannot delete directory %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) ldirdelete could not delete the specified directory. 2304 Argument %%$sub%%% is not a valid directory name. You must specify a valid directory name for ldircreate , ldirdelete , or $ldirlist . 2305 Cannot get the properties of %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) $lfileproperties could not retrieve the properties of the specified file or directory. 2306 Argument %%$sub%%% is not a valid file name. The specified argument for lfiledelete or lfilerename is not valid. 2307 Cannot delete file %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) lfiledelete or ldirdelete could not delete the specified file or directory. 2308 Cannot copy file %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) lfilecopy could not copy the specified file. 2309 Cannot move file %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) lfilemove could not move the specified file. 2310 Argument %%$sub%%% may not contain directory separators. The second argument for ldirrename or lfilerename must not contain directory separators. 2311 Cannot rename file %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) lfilerename or ldirrename could not rename the specified file or directory. 2312 Cannot list contents of %%$sub%%%: %%$sub(1) $ldirlist could not retrieve the contents of the specified directory.
